architecture.summary: Subspace-Aware Sparse Autoencoders (SASA) replace single-vector decoder directions in traditional SAEs with learned decoder subspaces. The system uses block sparsity via Top-s group gating and adapts each group's effective rank with nuclear-norm regularization. Information flows from model activations into a sparse latent code (block-sparse, gated by Top-s groups) whose groups are decoded via learned subspace projections rather than fixed directions, with regularization to control rank.
trained_components: decoder subspaces (learned basis matrices per group)

biblio.title: Subspace-Aware Sparse Autoencoders for Effective Mechanistic Interpretability

claims.statement: Reconstructing a feature of intrinsic dimension d_i ≥ 2 to error ε with single-direction decoders forces a number of atoms that is exponential in d_i

There exists a continuous path from the true d_i-dimensional basis to a strictly lower risk of the ℓ1-regularized SAE objective, whose descent directions drive any trained dictionary into the exponential regime
Once the block size satisfies r ≥ d_i, a single group not only can represent the entire feature slice but is the global minimizer of the SASA objective
SASA consolidation yields sample complexity polynomial in d_i rather than exponential
SASA reduces feature splitting and absorption, improves monosemanticity and interpretability
SASA matches or exceeds standard SAEs while training on roughly half the token budget
